title: cerastium arvense f. oblongifolium
intro: Cerastium arvense f. oblongifolium, also known as Field Chickweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense f. oblongifolium include treating skin issues such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as soothing digestive problems and reducing fever. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
